problems.name: Alcohol-related cirrhosis, decompensated
problems.notes: Decompensated since 2024 (ascites, hepatic encephalopathy, hepatic hydrothorax). MELD-Na in the high-20s at the 3/2027 presentation. Listed / being worked up for liver transplant. Sustained alcohol abstinence ~14 months.

problems.name: Hepatic encephalopathy
problems.notes: Recurrent; managed with lactulose (titrated to 3 BMs/day) and rifaximin. Episodic flares with unsteadiness/confusion.

problems.name: Diuretic-refractory ascites
problems.notes: Increasingly refractory. Attempts to up-titrate spironolactone + furosemide have been limited by renal injury and electrolyte derangement (see below). Requires recurrent large-volume paracentesis.

problems.name: Hepatic hydrothorax (right-sided)
problems.notes: Right-sided transudative effusion from cirrhosis. Causes dyspnea; requires recurrent therapeutic thoracentesis. Recurs quickly.

problems.name: Diuretic-induced acute kidney injury with hyponatremia and hyperkalemia
problems.notes: Recent hepatology attempts to increase furosemide and spironolactone to control the hydrothorax produced worsening creatinine, hyperkalemia, and hyponatremia — i.e., she does NOT tolerate higher diuretic doses. This is why she was referred for mechanical drainage (para/thora) rather than further diuresis.

problems.name: Thrombocytopenia and coagulopathy of chronic liver disease
problems.notes: Platelets chronically ~50-60 K/uL; INR chronically ~1.8-2.2 off any anticoagulation. Reflects synthetic dysfunction and splenic sequestration.

problems.name: Alcohol use disorder, in sustained remission
problems.notes: ~15 years of heavy use → cirrhosis. SOBER ~14 months, engaged in an outpatient recovery program + AA with documented abstinence. Supports transplant candidacy.

problems.name: Acute hypoxemic respiratory failure with pulmonary edema (4/2027)
problems.notes: Acute bilateral pulmonary edema requiring intubation; extubated the next day after IV diuresis. Occurred during the period of weekly outpatient thora/para (during which she received 4 units FFP plus albumin per session for an elevated INR). Added to the problem list after the 4/2027 ICU admission.

problems.name: Status post orthotopic liver transplant
problems.notes: Deceased-donor orthotopic liver transplant 7/19/2027 after sustained sobriety and completed evaluation. On immunosuppression. Ascites and hydrothorax have essentially resolved (no longer requires recurrent drainage).
